docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Congratulations on reaching the deepest depths of the Ultrabroken rabbit hole! Below is valuable information regarding individual Ultrabroken Effects for various Zonai Devices. While some effects prove to be more advantageous than others, here is a list of the currently known Ultrabroken Effects on individual Zonai Devices.

docs.title: Movement & Transport
docs.text:
• Fan - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimation
  • Wing - Consistency, Permanence
  • Cart - Consistency, Permanence, Very slidy
  • Balloon - Consistency, Permanence, Disanimation (Needs more testing)
  • Rocket - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Disanimation (Needs more testing)
  • Sled - (Needs more testing)
  • Big Wheel - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Autoflux quirks, Disanimation, Antimass effects
  • Small Wheel - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimated to inactive
  • Steering Stick - Cancels Autoflux of some Ultrabroken devices (Needs testing)

docs.title: Hazards & Effects
docs.text:
• Time Bomb - Consistency, Permanence, Disanimation
  • Flame Emitter - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimated to inactive
  • Frost Emitter - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimated to inactive
  • Shock Emitter - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Disanimation, Can get stuck
  • Beam Emitter - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Disanimation
  • Hydrant - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimated to inactive

docs.title: Utilities & Support
docs.text:
• Portable Pot - Consistency, Permanence, Active Autoflux, Disanimation, Disjointed by Antimass
  • Spring - Consistency, Permanence, Disjointed by Antimass
  • Cannon - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimated to inactive
  • Stabilizer - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimation, Inherent Antigravity
  • Hover Stone - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimation, Inherent Antigravity, Defusable by throwing
  • Light - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimation, Can get stuck
  • Mirror - Consistency, Permanence, Active Autoflux, Disanimation (Needs more testing)

docs.title: Power Systems
docs.text:
• Battery - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Limitless Flux, Disanimation
  • Big Battery - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Limitless Flux, Disanimation

docs.title: Special Devices
docs.text:
• Stake - Can't be displaced by Ultrabroken (Needs more testing)
  • Homing Cart - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux, Disanimation
  • Construct Head - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux (socket), Inactive Autoflux (head), Disanimation, Disjointed by Antimass
  • Shrine Propeller - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux(?), Disanimation(?), Wind gusts get stuck
  • Shrine Motor - Consistency, Permanence, Self-Sufficiency, Active Autoflux(?), Disanimation, Antimass effects

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Battery

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Place a Zonai Battery in front the Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on top the Zonai Battery
  3. Activate the Zonai Battery
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the Zonai Battery
  8. Check if the Zonai Battery is Ultrabroken
  9. Connect any battery-powered Zonai System to the Ultrabroken Zonai Battery

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Ultrabroken Object
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an Ultrabroken Object is nearby
  • Feed the Ultrabroken Object to a lifeform that devours objects
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

Related: Quantum State Freeze for understanding these effects.

docs.title: Ultrabroken Effects
docs.text: While Ultrabroken may seem as simple as a single dialogue, it actually opens up a profound rabbit hole. Within this realm, numerous mind-bending outcomes and effects can be achieved through specific, and at times rather intricate, configurations.

docs.title: Important Note
docs.text: Ultrabroken Effects are not limited to the objects used in the following setups and they can be stacked, so feel free to experiment with any kind or number of objects and combine effects at whim.

docs.title: Core Effects
docs.text:
• Wacko Boingo - Ultrabroken Flight
  • Lift Lock - When systems become impossible to lift
  • Recall Fling - Objects flung by Recall
  • Ghost Glue - Invisible attraction connections
  • Remnant Glue - Ghost Glue remnants

docs.title: Advanced Effects
docs.text:
• Glue Lock - When Ultrahand Glue breaks
  • Mass Matching - Understanding object interactions
  • Ghost Smuggle - Portable Ghost Glue systems
  • Phantom Smuggle - Ultra-portable systems
  • Resurrect Smuggle - In development
  • Ouija Entanglement - Velocity linking

docs.title: Quantum Effects
docs.text:
• Antimass - Weightless objects
  • Freecall - In development
  • Quantum State Freeze - Frozen states

docs.title: Specialized Effects
docs.text:
• Fuse Stacking - Equipment combinations
  • Transmigration - Item soul removal
  • Autobuild Standalones - Detachable systems
  • Zonai Device Hacks - Individual device effects
  • Slidy Cart - Semi-Ultrabroken friction override

Start with: Wacko Boingo - the most popular effect!

docs.title: Antimass
docs.text: Discovered by Lafoscony

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
As we delve deeper into the realm of Ultrabroken Effects, we encounter the fascinating Antimass. If gravity is dampening the enjoyment, Antimass comes to the rescue, causing objects to float in mesmerizing weightless fashion and disjoining objects comprised of multiple parts.

By annihilating mass entirely, we can construct highly efficient and amusing UltraFuses when combined with Spooky Interactions. Provided they don't fly off into space.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Cooking Pot

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
1. Place a Cooking Pot in front the Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on top the Cooking Pot
  3. Recall the Cooking Pot
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the Cooking Pot
  8. Check if the Cooking Pot is Ultrabroken

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Ultrabroken Object
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an Ultrabroken Object is nearby
  • Feed the Ultrabroken Object to a lifeform that devours objects
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

Related: Quantum State Freeze includes Antimass interactions.

docs.title: Autobuild Standalones
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text: As the madness slowly reaches its crescendo, Ultrabroken unveils a highly intriguing effect on Autobuild Zonai Systems. These systems can now be detached into individual standalone objects, expanding our creative possibilities, especially regarding equipment and materials.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • An Autobuild Zonai System

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Place your Autobuild Zonai System in front of the Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on top the Autobuild Zonai System
  3. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  4. Finish the dialogue
  5. Let the cutscene do its magic
  6. Find the Autobuild Zonai System
  7. Check if the Autobuild Zonai System is Ultrabroken
  8. Keep detaching

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Semi-Ultrabroken Object
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while a Semi-Ultrabroken Object is nearby
  • Expose the Semi-Ultrabroken Object to a shockwave that despawns objects
  • Feed the Semi-Ultrabroken Object to a lifeform that devours objects
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

Related: Zonai Device Hacks and Slidy Cart.

docs.title: Fuse Stacking
docs.text: Discovered by Trli

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Fancy text coming soon.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • 3 shields
  • 3 wooden planks

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Place your shield in front the Riverside Trolls
  2. Glue a wooden plank on top of the shield
  3. Glue another shield on top of the wooden plank
  4. Glue another wooden plank on top of the shield
  5. Repeat steps 3 and 4
  6. Unglue everything and make sure all objects are stacking on top of each other!
  7. Step on top the topmost wooden plank
  8. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  9. Finish the dialogue
  10. Let the cutscene do its magic
  11. Find the shield
  12. Check if the shields are Ultrabroken
  13. Pick up an Ultrabroken Shield
  14. Fuse an Ultrabroken Shield to it
  15. Drop the Ultrabroken Shield-Shield
  16. Pick up the last Ultrabroken Shield
  17. Fuse the Ultrabroken Shield-Shield to it

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory without equipping it, put it on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Ultrabroken Object
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an Ultrabroken Object is nearby
  • Feed the Ultrabroken Object to a lifeform that devours objects
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

docs.title: Ghost Glue
docs.text: Discovered by Duncan

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The Ghost Glue is an exceptionally potent Ultrabroken Effect and Spooky Interaction that enables us to establish connections between objects in unintended and eerie manners. It introduces the mechanics of Attraction, while simultaneously amplifying the significance of Zonai System Arrangement and Mass Matching.

Key Characteristics:

  • No Ultrahand Glue - Objects are held together by Attraction alone
  • Cannot be undone - Ghost Glue connections cannot be severed by conventional means
  • Stability depends on Mass Matching - The relative masses of objects determine connection strength
  • No Flux transfer - Ghost Glued Zonai Systems do not transfer Flux to one another
  • Doesn't count toward glue limit - Ghost Glue connections do not count towards the internal maximum glue connections counter

This effect is also known as "Duncan's Ghost Glue."

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Cart
  • A second Zonai Cart

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on the Zonai Cart
  3. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  4. Finish the dialogue
  5. Let the cutscene do its magic
  6. Find the Zonai Cart
  7. Check if the Zonai Cart is Ultrabroken
  8. Put a second regular Zonai Cart next to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  9. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart to the regular Zonai Cart

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory without equipping it, put it on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory to cancel it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel it
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel it
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Related: See Mass Matching for understanding Ghost Glue connections, or Ghost Smuggle for advanced techniques.

docs.title: Ghost Smuggle
docs.text: Discovered by The_Andromeda, Lafoscony

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
If you've reached this point: Congratulations! You're halfway there, and now the fun part begins! Ghost Smuggling is an amusing technique that involves utilizing Ghost Glue between Ultrabroken Equipment and various objects that can be glued together. From this stage onward, it becomes crucial to consider Mass Matching.

This technique is also commonly referred to as "Ghost Glue Smuggling".

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Cart
  • A shield

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls
  2. Glue a shield to the Zonai Cart
  3. Step on the Zonai Cart
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the Zonai Cart
  8. Check if the Zonai Cart is Ultrabroken
  9. Pick up the Ultrabroken Shield while having no other shield equipped
  10. Drop the Ultrabroken Shield next to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  11. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Shield to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  12. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Cart to the Ultrabroken Shield
  13. Pick up the Ultrabroken Shield
  14. Start moving

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Drop the Ultrabroken Equipment to cancel the Ghost Smuggle
  • Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory without equipping it, put it on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory to cancel it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel it
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel it
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Related: Phantom Smuggle - even more powerful!

docs.title: Glue Lock
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The most vexing Ultrabroken Effect completely disables the Ultrahand Ability, rendering Ghost Glue inaccessible for Ultrabroken Objects, or in some cases, preventing any objects from being glued at all. Discovering the solution to this issue may offer a clue as to its underlying cause.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Ultrahand Glue
  • Ultrabroken Objects

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text: 1. Use regular Ultrahand Glue mechanics on Ultrabroken Objects

docs.title: Fix A
docs.text: 1. Unglue the object that was glued last (?)

docs.title: Fix B
docs.text: • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Remnant Glue

docs.title: Fix C
docs.text: • Glue over the last Ghost Glue connection and detach it again

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory without equipping it, put it on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory to cancel it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel it
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel it
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Related: Remnant Glue may be the underlying cause.

docs.title: Lift Lock
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
There's a significant likelihood that you have encountered this rather disadvantageous Ultrabroken Effect. Lift Locks render entire Zonai Systems, containing Ultrabroken Objects, impossible to lift. The cause of this issue is remarkably easy to overlook and avoid, but rectifying it becomes considerably more challenging in intricate scenarios.

Key Information: Lift Locks will occur every time Link steps on a regular object that is part of a Zonai System containing an Ultrabroken Object. Zonai Systems constructed with Ghost Glue, however, remain unaffected by this particular Ultrabroken Effect.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Cart
  • A second Zonai Cart

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on the Zonai Cart
  3. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  4. Finish the dialogue
  5. Let the cutscene do its magic
  6. Find the Zonai Cart
  7. Check if the Zonai Cart is Ultrabroken
  8. Glue a regular Zonai Cart to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  9. Step on the regular Zonai Cart
  10. Step back on the ground
  11. Ultrahand the regular Zonai Cart
  12. Apply directional pull

docs.title: The Fix
docs.text:
If you encounter a Lift Lock:

  1. Unglue the regular Zonai Cart from the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  2. Glue the regular Zonai Cart back to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  3. Ultrahand the regular Zonai Cart
  4. Apply directional pull

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Ultrabroken Object
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an Ultrabroken Object is nearby
  • Feed the Ultrabroken Object to a lifeform that devours objects
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

Up Next: Recall Fling

docs.title: Mass Matching
docs.text: Discovered by The_Andromeda, Lafoscony, optimized by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
When attempting to establish a Ghost Glue connection between two objects, it is crucial to comprehend the mass properties of the objects involved and bear in mind a set of rules that determine whether the Ghost Glue connection will be loose or taut.

Weaker Ghost Glue connections are commonly referred to as "Puppet Glue" and "Puppet Smuggle".

docs.title: General Rules
docs.text:
• Ultrabroken Objects with low mass have low Attraction to regular objects and Ultrabroken Objects with low mass
  • Ultrabroken Objects with high mass have high Attraction to regular objects and Ultrabroken Objects with high mass
  • Ultrabroken Objects with high mass have low Attraction to regular objects and Ultrabroken Objects with low mass
  • The total net mass of all parts on each end of a Ghost Glue connection is crucial for its stability

docs.title: Ultrabroken-Regular Interactions
docs.text:
• Ultrabroken Objects with low mass have low Attraction to regular Objects with high mass
  • Ultrabroken Objects with low mass have higher Attraction to regular Objects with low mass if those are Ultrahanded directly or indirectly
  • Ultrabroken Objects with high mass have very high Attraction to regular Objects with low mass if those are Ultrahanded directly or indirectly

docs.title: Ultrabroken-Ultrabroken Interactions
docs.text:
• Ultrabroken Objects with low mass have high Attraction to Ultrabroken Objects with high mass
  • The Attraction between two Ultrabroken Objects is higher when Ghost Glue comes from both sides

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Cart
  • Another Zonai Cart
  • A Material
  • Another Material

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls
  2. Glue a Material to the Zonai Cart
  3. Step on the Zonai Cart
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the Zonai Cart
  8. Check if the Zonai Cart is Ultrabroken
  9. Detach the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart from the Ultrabroken Material
  10. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart to the Ultrabroken Material
  11. Glue a regular Material to the Ultrabroken Material
  12. Move whole thing up and rotate it quickly using Ultrahand on the regular Material until the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart falls off
  13. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Material to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  14. Move whole thing up and rotate it quickly using Ultrahand on the regular Material
  15. Detach the regular Material
  16. Pick up the Ultrabroken Material
  17. Put a regular Zonai Cart next to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  18. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart to the regular Zonai Cart
  19. Put the regular Material on top of the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  20. Ghost Glue the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart to the regular Material
  21. Lift the whole thing up and rotate it quickly using Ultrahand on the regular Zonai Cart until the regular Material falls off
  22. Lift the whole thing up and rotate it quickly using Ultrahand on the regular Material

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory unequipped, on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory to cancel it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel it
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel it
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Essential for: Ghost Smuggle and advanced techniques.

docs.title: Ouija Entanglement
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
This subtle Spooky Interaction often goes unnoticed during Glue-Less Ultrabroken. The velocities of the objects involved seem to exhibit peculiar interconnections, resembling the movement patterns of an Ouija marker attempting to transmit messages from the realm beyond.

While there may not be any significant untapped potential in this phenomenon, it consistently occurs whenever Glue-Free Ultrabroken is executed. It's also possible to set up Ouija Entanglement between a regular object and an Ultrabroken one, by utilizing Fuse Entanglement Protection.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A shield
  • A Zonai Cart

docs.title: Initialization
docs.text:
1. Place your shield in front of the Riverside Trolls
  2. Place the Zonai Cart on top of the shield - Do not glue anything!
  3. Step on top the Zonai Cart
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the shield
  8. Check if the shield is Ultrabroken
  9. Move Ultrabroken Shield around

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory without equipping it, remove a fuse on it, put it on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory or restore it to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel the Spooky Interaction
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel the Spooky Interaction

Related: Phantom Smuggle also uses Glue-Less Ultrabroken.

docs.title: Phantom Smuggle
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Phantom Smuggling revolves around a fascinating Spooky Interaction known as Phantom Glue, which can be described as Ghost Glue on crack. This Ultrabroken Effect enables the creation of highly portable and fully functional Zonai Systems that are securely attached to equipped Ultrabroken Equipment.

Key Differences from Ghost Glue:

  • Includes Ultrahand Glue - Phantom Glue has actual glue connections
  • More rigid - Objects are held tighter than with Ghost Glue
  • Can only originate from regular objects - Not from Ultrabroken objects
  • Can be unglued - Phantom Glue can be severed like normal glue

The setup for Phantom Smuggle introduces the concept of Glue-less Ultrabroken.

Also known as "Phantom Glue Smuggling".

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A shield
  • A Zonai Cart
  • An apple

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Place your shield in front of the Riverside Trolls
  2. Place the Zonai Cart on top of the shield - Do not glue anything!
  3. Step on top the Zonai Cart
  4. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the shield
  8. Check if the shield is Ultrabroken
  9. Glue an apple to the Ultrabroken Shield
  10. Pick up the Ultrabroken Shield

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
• Detach objects glued to the Ultrabroken Equipment to cancel Phantom Smuggle
  • Put Ultrabroken Equipment into the inventory unequipped, on a mount or give it to the Break Apart shop to restore it
  • Put an object involved in a Spooky Interaction into the inventory to cancel it
  • Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Trigger a dialogue or cutscene that deletes objects while an object involved in a Spooky Interaction is nearby to cancel it
  • Feed one object involved in a Spooky Interaction to a lifeform that devours objects to cancel it
  • Warp anywhere without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Related: Ghost Smuggle for a comparison, or Transmigration for equipment fusing.

docs.title: Quantum State Freeze
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Quantum State Freeze encompasses a diverse range of chilling Ultrabroken Effects. Consequently, the individual effects it exerts on objects will be collectively known as Quantum State Freeze Effects.

docs.title: Consistency (Dura Freeze)
docs.text:
Consistency encompasses a series of powerful Quantum State Freeze Effects:

  • Unalterable durability - Durability of Ultrabroken Bows, Ultrabroken Shields, and specific fuses on Ultrabroken Weapons cannot decrease over time
  • Boss-proof - Ultrabroken Objects can withstand shockwaves from Lynels and Bosses, effectively transforming them into perfect traps
  • Flame resistance - Wooden Ultrabroken Objects with Consistency cannot be consumed by flames and will remain perpetually ablaze
  • Destructible shells - Destructible Ultrabroken Materials can be destroyed, leaving behind an empty yet functional shell
  • ChuChu Alchemy - ChuChu Jellies cannot be transmuted, allowing Green ChuChu Jellies, for example, to burn indefinitely

docs.title: Permanence
docs.text: Zonai Devices possess inherent lifetimes of approximately 15 minutes, their countdown clock constantly ticking away. However, the Quantum State Freeze Effect Permanence has the power to halt this timer. With permanent Ultrabroken Zonai Devices, you can rely on their unwavering functionality throughout extended sessions without the fear of their lifespan expiring.

docs.title: Self-Sufficiency
docs.text:
Among the intriguing Quantum State Freeze Effects associated with Ultrabroken Zonai Devices, one stands out prominently: Self-Sufficiency. Ultrabroken Zonai Devices possess the remarkable ability to operate without draining batteries whatsoever, rendering them self-sustaining in this regard.

Furthermore, there exist specific Zonai Devices that go even further, supplying infinite amounts of Flux to connected Zonai Systems, amplifying their functionality indefinitely.

docs.title: Autoflux
docs.text:
Another intriguing Quantum State Freeze Effect observed in Ultrabroken Zonai Devices is Autoflux. It exhibits variation among individual devices, with some permanently activated and others permanently deactivated.

The activation state of Ultrabroken Zonai Devices becomes fixed and cannot be toggled anymore. Moreover, certain devices even transmit their erratic Autoflux behavior to connected Zonai Devices, further complicating their interactions.

docs.title: Disanimation
docs.text: Numerous Ultrabroken Zonai Devices exhibiting this effect remain locked in the animation they were in when Ultrabroken was performed on them. Some animations leave Zonai Devices not working properly after Ultrabroken has been performed on them while those animations were playing.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Shock Emitter

docs.text:

  2. Step on top the Shock Emitter
  3. Activate the Shock Emitter

  5. Finish the dialogue
  6. Let the cutscene do its magic
  7. Find the Shock Emitter
  8. Check if the Shock Emitter is Ultrabroken

• Exceed the Distance Unload Radius of the Ultrabroken Object

Related: See Zonai Device Hacks for per-device breakdowns of these effects.

docs.title: Recall Fling
docs.text: Discovered by Lafoscony, optimized by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The Recall Fling is yet another seemingly disadvantageous Ultrabroken Effect. It arises when an Ultrabroken Object that has another object attached to it is subjected to the Recall ability. However, there are several exceptions to this rule, with one of the most notable being the case of Steering Sticks when Link is standing on them.

docs.text:

1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls

docs.title: Remnant Glue
docs.text: Discovered by Duncan

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
A mysterious phenomenon known as a Spooky Interaction pops in and out of existence wherever Ghost Glue is tampered with. While it can often be disregarded, certain outcomes suggest that particular Remnant Glue arrangements are responsible for Glue Locks.

Moreover, there is a belief that Remnant Glue from previous Ultrabroken sessions can unexpectedly reappear in future Ultrabroken sessions, adding complexity to the situation.

docs.text:
• Access to The Riverside Trolls
  • A Zonai Cart

docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart in front of The Riverside Trolls
  2. Step on the object
  3. Talk to one of The Riverside Trolls
  4. Finish the dialogue
  5. Let the cutscene do its magic
  6. Find the object
  7. Check if the Zonai Cart is Ultrabroken
  8. Put the regular Zonai Cart next to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  9. Glue the regular Zonai Cart to the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  10. Ultrahand the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart
  11. Detach the Ultrabroken Zonai Cart

  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on both objects involved in a Spooky Interaction to cancel it

Related: See Glue Lock which may be caused by Remnant Glue issues.

docs.title: Slidy Cart (Wacko Wheels)
docs.text: Discovered by Duncan, optimized by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Shortly after the discovery of Ultrabroken, a Semi-Ultrabroken Effect emerged, which involves overriding the friction properties of Cart wheels in particular. Often referred to as "Wacko Wheels," this effect operates independently of The Riverside Trolls, making it a suitable companion for Ultrabroken Equipment.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• A Zonai Cart
  • Access to a stable
  • A horse
  • Access to horse gear

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Put a Zonai Cart sideways right in front of any stable's counter
  2. Step on the Zonai Cart
  3. Talk to the stable receptionist
  4. Choose to change horse gear
  5. Quit out of the dialogue
  6. Put the Semi-Ultrabroken Zonai Cart on top of a hill
  7. Quickly step on it

  • Transition into another map without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object
  • Load another save without performing Zuggle Load Object Transfer on the Ultrabroken Object

Note: This doesn't require The Riverside Trolls and can be combined with other Ultrabroken Equipment!

docs.title: Transmigration
docs.text: Discovered by NaN Gogh

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
As we approach the depths of our exploration, we encounter the Transmigration effect, which grants us the ability to temporarily remove the Item Soul from items, rendering them uncollectible. This ability proves to be a real lifesaver for UltraFuses that involve items or equipment.

Various methods involving Antimass and the Quantum State Freeze Effect Consistency can trigger this Ultrabroken Effect. However, there is one specific case where Transmigration becomes particularly useful, though the specifics may vary.

  10. Pick up the Ultrabroken Shield

  12. Press + again

docs.title: Smuggling and Zuggling
docs.text: Basic stub

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Smuggling and Zuggling are object-transfer and placement glitches. They let players move, hide, duplicate, or desynchronize equipment between zones and saves by abusing item pickup/drop/swap, general equipment handling, and engine state transitions.

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text: • Smuggling and Zuggling Basic setups Smuggling and Zuggling

docs.title: What to expect in each writeup
docs.text:
• Trigger: how to reproduce (inputs, positions, equipment)
  • Requirements: game version, objects, NPCs, devices
  • Behavior: observable effects and common outcomes
  • Risks & Warnings: save/desync risks and suggested safety steps
  • Notes variations, related techniques, and links to follow-ups

docs.title: Safety & best practices
docs.text:
• Always test on throwaway saves or copies. These glitches can corrupt progress.
  • Prefer manual backups (copy / cloud-backup the save file) rather than relying on in-game saves.
  • Use the writeups in this index to compare variants before attempting advanced chains.
